title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston events.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
Chianca's background includes roles as a news editor, columnist, and music writer.

title: How well do you know your World Cup trivia? Take our quiz.
author: Hayden Bird and Kelly Chan
bullets: Boston.com offers a World Cup trivia quiz to test readers' soccer knowledge ahead of upcoming matches.
The first World Cup match at Gillette Stadium is scheduled in a few weeks.
Eric Wynalda's 1994 World Cup goal against Switzerland at the Silverdome is highlighted in the quiz.

title: 9 students brought to hospital after school bus crash in Everett
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Nine of 11 students on a school bus were sent to hospitals after a crash in Everett for observation.
The accident occurred at Broadway and Langton Street involving a school bus and a black car; investigation ongoing.
Surveillance footage shows the bus crossing a busy street when a black car hit its side; no serious injuries reported.

title: Huge endangered snapping turtle, somehow loose in Mass., rescued from pond
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A rare alligator snapping turtle was found in an Essex County pond, illegally abandoned by its owner.
The 60-pound turtle, not native to Massachusetts, was lethargic and suffering from pneumonia due to cold water.
The turtle is being treated at New England Wildlife Center and may return to its native range for conservation.

title: After state trooper killed, Senate approves amendment aimed at deterring wrong-way drivers
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts Senate voted 40-0 to mandate new infrastructure and warning systems against wrong-way drivers.
The amendment prioritizes public safety with measures like directional striping, signage, and detection systems.
Trooper Kevin Trainor was killed in a head-on collision by a wrong-way driver on Route 1 in Lynnfield.

title: Thoughts on owning one gas and one electric vehicle
author: John Paul
bullets: AAA's Car Doctor suggests a hybrid garage with one gas and one electric car for versatile needs.
The Hyundai Ioniq models are praised for their performance; Tesla Model 3 was not fully evaluated.
Home charging options like the MSI Smart Charger enhance the practicality of electric vehicle ownership.

title: Red Sox top prospect Franklin Arias makes major leap in latest ESPN rankings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Franklin Arias jumped from No. 36 to No. 6 in ESPN’s top 50 prospect rankings.
Arias has a .328 batting average with eight home runs and 25 RBIs for Double-A Portland.
The 20-year-old was signed from Venezuela in 2023 and is projected as a future MLB shortstop.

title: Police respond to threat made against Perkins School for the Blind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Watertown police responded to an online threat against Perkins School for the Blind, prompting a lockdown Thursday morning.
The threat to Perkins School for the Blind was assessed and resolved with no charges filed, police confirmed.
While the situation is resolved, police will not release further details due to student privacy considerations.

title: Second arrest made in alleged sexual abuse of dog in Connecticut
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Damon Mesite, 28, was arrested in Meriden, Connecticut, for aiding and abetting sexual assault of an animal.
Kristiina Kennedy, 45, of Meriden, was earlier charged with sexually assaulting her dog and producing pornography.
Kennedy's dog and cats were taken by animal control; authorities found videos suggesting illegal acts with the animals.

title: Bruins reportedly moving on from one of their assistant coaches
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are reportedly not renewing assistant coach Jay Leach's contract for the upcoming season.
Leach rejoined the Bruins in June 2024, focusing on defense and penalty kill under coaches Jim Montgomery and Marco Sturm.
Under Leach, Bruins ranked 14th in goals allowed per game but struggled in penalty kill and high-danger scoring chances.
